mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns remain uncorrelated with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, forcing firms to cut capital expenditures and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive or flat 20-day forward returns, or the mechanism shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between rising core inflation and a static federal funds rate signals delayed monetary policy response, prompting equity market repricing and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.3% while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged
failure_condition: The inflation-rate gap fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with a positive Rank IC over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% confirms an economic downturn, triggering immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in > 70% of quarters following a GDPC1 contraction > 0.5%, or the signal exhibits no predictive alpha.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production expansion during a post-shock recovery phase indicates supply chain normalization and margin improvement, driving positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO surges during recovery phases fail to generate positive 5-day returns, or the mechanism loses predictive power when tested against a buy-and-hold benchmark.
